construction: 12 exact cases across minimal-evidence recurrence inference, 12-job constrained optimization, 17-person nested logic

cases.prompt:
This sequence is generated by a periodic recurrence over modular arithmetic. For n >= 4: a[n] = (p[n mod 4]*a[n-1] + q[n mod 4]*a[n-2] + t*a[n-3] + r*n + s) mod M. The unknown modulus M is exactly one of 97, 101, or 103. The 8 periodic coefficients p[0..3], q[0..3] are each one of -2, -1, 1, or 2. The global coefficient t is also one of those four values. The coefficient r is a nonzero integer from -4 through 4, and s is an integer from -4 through 4. Remainders are reported in the range 0 through M-1. Indexing begins at n=1. Infer and verify the unique system, then return the next 3 terms.

Assign twelve compute jobs to machines W, X, Y, and Z. Each machine must receive exactly three jobs. Minimize total cost while satisfying every interaction. The optimum is unique, but several feasible assignments are close in cost. Return the complete assignment and exact total cost.

Job | W | X | Y | Z
--- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---:
J1 | 28 | 13 | 22 | 18
J2 | 26 | 18 | 28 | 28
J3 | 6 | 32 | 35 | 30
J4 | 23 | 1 | 1 | 11
J5 | 4 | 9 | 34 | 23
J6 | 34 | 25 | 4 | 32
J7 | 12 | 8 | 19 | 5
J8 | 21 | 11 | 32 | 34
J9 | 9 | 30 | 4 | 8
J10 | 32 | 11 | 26 | 10
J11 | 29 | 18 | 24 | 14
J12 | 1 | 33 | 13 | 32

Constraints:
- J8 and J10 must use different machines.
- J5 and J12 must use different machines.
- J4 and J5 must use different machines.
- J2 and J11 must use the same machine.
- J6 may use only Y or Z.
- J3 may use only X or Z.
- J5 may not use Y.
- J7 may not use Y.
- If J4 uses W, then J2 may not use X.
- If J12 uses Y, then J1 may not use Y.
- If J10 uses Y, then J9 may not use X.
- If J8 uses Z, then J11 may not use X.
- Exactly one of J7, J4, and J2 must use X.
- Exactly one of J7, J6, and J2 must use W.

Each person is either truthful or a liar. A truthful person's entire nested statement is true, while a liar's entire statement is false. T(X) means X is truthful. AND, OR, NOT, XOR, IFF, and IMPLIES have their standard Boolean meanings; OR is inclusive. EXACTLY(k; ...) is true when exactly k listed expressions are true. Determine the unique type of every person.

- A says: "XOR(XOR(T(B), T(C)), EXACTLY(2; T(N); T(C); T(K)))."
- B says: "AND(NOT(T(O)), AND(T(H), T(C)))."
- C says: "OR(XOR(T(E), T(F)), XOR(T(L), T(I)))."
- D says: "AND(EXACTLY(1; T(F); T(H); T(L)), XOR(T(E), T(P)))."
- E says: "IFF(AND(AND(T(B), T(H)), EXACTLY(1; T(Q); T(P); T(O))), IMPLIES(OR(T(P), T(H)), AND(T(C), T(M))))."
- F says: "XOR(IFF(OR(T(P), T(M)), AND(T(A), T(Q))), IFF(NOT(T(J)), NOT(T(A))))."
- G says: "IFF(XOR(NOT(T(M)), OR(T(K), T(J))), IMPLIES(T(C), NOT(T(L))))."
- H says: "IMPLIES(AND(T(K), EXACTLY(1; T(I); T(C); T(N))), AND(T(P), NOT(T(L))))."
- I says: "XOR(IMPLIES(NOT(T(O)), T(J)), OR(T(F), T(C)))."
- J says: "XOR(AND(AND(T(P), T(A)), T(B)), IMPLIES(EXACTLY(1; T(Q); T(C); T(G)), EXACTLY(1; T(H); T(O); T(C))))."
- K says: "OR(XOR(T(J), T(O)), OR(T(I), T(O)))."
- L says: "AND(IMPLIES(XOR(T(F), T(Q)), NOT(T(N))), IMPLIES(XOR(T(N), T(A)), AND(T(N), T(J))))."
- M says: "XOR(OR(T(N), T(C)), EXACTLY(2; T(K); T(E); T(O)))."
- N says: "OR(OR(OR(T(J), T(C)), AND(T(M), T(G))), IFF(OR(T(L), T(I)), OR(T(J), T(M))))."
- O says: "XOR(IMPLIES(AND(T(H), T(K)), T(K)), XOR(XOR(T(E), T(A)), AND(T(L), T(I))))."
- P says: "IFF(IMPLIES(T(N), EXACTLY(2; T(F); T(Q); T(I))), XOR(T(M), T(D)))."
- Q says: "IFF(XOR(T(C), EXACTLY(2; T(M); T(E); T(I))), AND(OR(T(B), T(K)), OR(T(J), T(A))))."

Each person is either truthful or a liar. A truthful person's entire nested statement is true, while a liar's entire statement is false. T(X) means X is truthful. AND, OR, NOT, XOR, IFF, and IMPLIES have their standard Boolean meanings; OR is inclusive. EXACTLY(k; ...) is true when exactly k listed expressions are true. Determine the unique type of every person.

- A says: "AND(AND(OR(T(M), T(O)), AND(T(E), T(K))), AND(T(F), T(E)))."
- B says: "IMPLIES(EXACTLY(2; T(C); T(A); T(O)), AND(T(J), T(P)))."
- C says: "XOR(NOT(T(F)), AND(T(I), T(L)))."
- D says: "IMPLIES(XOR(AND(T(C), T(E)), T(O)), IFF(XOR(T(H), T(L)), XOR(T(H), T(I))))."
- E says: "IMPLIES(AND(OR(T(C), T(M)), T(C)), AND(AND(T(P), T(C)), OR(T(O), T(C))))."
- F says: "XOR(IFF(T(A), AND(T(D), T(A))), AND(OR(T(M), T(N)), OR(T(A), T(I))))."
- G says: "XOR(OR(T(P), T(C)), AND(T(O), T(Q)))."
- H says: "XOR(EXACTLY(2; T(K); T(D); T(F)), AND(T(L), T(K)))."
- I says: "IFF(XOR(T(B), T(O)), EXACTLY(2; T(G); T(P); T(A)))."
- J says: "AND(IFF(OR(T(G), T(C)), EXACTLY(1; T(G); T(E); T(N))), IMPLIES(EXACTLY(1; T(D); T(F); T(O)), EXACTLY(2; T(P); T(G); T(L))))."
- K says: "OR(IFF(T(E), AND(T(G), T(D))), XOR(OR(T(P), T(B)), AND(T(M), T(E))))."
- L says: "AND(IFF(OR(T(A), T(Q)), T(Q)), IMPLIES(NOT(T(G)), EXACTLY(1; T(N); T(C); T(G))))."
- M says: "IMPLIES(AND(NOT(T(H)), NOT(T(F))), IFF(EXACTLY(2; T(D); T(K); T(C)), OR(T(L), T(N))))."
- N says: "IMPLIES(OR(OR(T(O), T(B)), OR(T(Q), T(B))), AND(OR(T(F), T(P)), AND(T(A), T(M))))."
- O says: "IFF(AND(OR(T(N), T(J)), T(E)), AND(AND(T(E), T(F)), OR(T(E), T(A))))."
- P says: "IFF(AND(T(M), T(J)), XOR(T(B), T(C)))."
- Q says: "AND(XOR(XOR(T(A), T(O)), XOR(T(D), T(P))), XOR(T(F), EXACTLY(2; T(H); T(J); T(N))))."

Each person is either truthful or a liar. A truthful person's entire nested statement is true, while a liar's entire statement is false. T(X) means X is truthful. AND, OR, NOT, XOR, IFF, and IMPLIES have their standard Boolean meanings; OR is inclusive. EXACTLY(k; ...) is true when exactly k listed expressions are true. Determine the unique type of every person.

- A says: "XOR(OR(NOT(T(O)), XOR(T(D), T(H))), IFF(AND(T(C), T(L)), OR(T(N), T(P))))."
- B says: "OR(T(I), AND(T(G), T(N)))."
- C says: "AND(EXACTLY(2; T(O); T(K); T(J)), OR(T(A), T(K)))."
- D says: "IMPLIES(IFF(T(O), NOT(T(F))), IMPLIES(AND(T(K), T(F)), EXACTLY(2; T(L); T(C); T(I))))."
- E says: "OR(NOT(T(L)), XOR(T(K), T(C)))."
- F says: "IFF(T(Q), EXACTLY(2; T(O); T(L); T(C)))."
- G says: "IMPLIES(NOT(T(F)), EXACTLY(2; T(N); T(C); T(L)))."
- H says: "XOR(AND(EXACTLY(1; T(J); T(P); T(Q)), XOR(T(I), T(A))), IFF(OR(T(I), T(M)), NOT(T(Q))))."
- I says: "IFF(IMPLIES(NOT(T(B)), XOR(T(C), T(G))), IFF(AND(T(F), T(D)), XOR(T(A), T(F))))."
- J says: "AND(XOR(NOT(T(K)), XOR(T(G), T(H))), IMPLIES(NOT(T(L)), AND(T(G), T(E))))."
- K says: "AND(IMPLIES(AND(T(F), T(O)), NOT(T(Q))), AND(EXACTLY(2; T(G); T(A); T(M)), T(O)))."
- L says: "OR(EXACTLY(2; T(N); T(G); T(I)), NOT(T(C)))."
- M says: "IMPLIES(AND(OR(T(L), T(E)), OR(T(H), T(L))), XOR(T(A), XOR(T(J), T(A))))."
- N says: "IMPLIES(IMPLIES(EXACTLY(2; T(E); T(B); T(H)), OR(T(B), T(M))), OR(AND(T(L), T(C)), XOR(T(C), T(L))))."
- O says: "OR(XOR(XOR(T(Q), T(G)), EXACTLY(2; T(P); T(F); T(Q))), IFF(XOR(T(B), T(F)), T(J)))."
- P says: "IMPLIES(IMPLIES(AND(T(M), T(J)), OR(T(F), T(H))), IMPLIES(EXACTLY(1; T(M); T(C); T(L)), NOT(T(F))))."
- Q says: "XOR(IFF(T(H), OR(T(O), T(M))), OR(T(J), EXACTLY(1; T(O); T(B); T(A))))."

Each person is either truthful or a liar. A truthful person's entire nested statement is true, while a liar's entire statement is false. T(X) means X is truthful. AND, OR, NOT, XOR, IFF, and IMPLIES have their standard Boolean meanings; OR is inclusive. EXACTLY(k; ...) is true when exactly k listed expressions are true. Determine the unique type of every person.

- A says: "IFF(AND(EXACTLY(2; T(C); T(M); T(O)), AND(T(O), T(L))), OR(OR(T(K), T(O)), XOR(T(M), T(Q))))."
- B says: "IMPLIES(XOR(T(E), T(Q)), AND(T(N), T(F)))."
- C says: "IMPLIES(OR(NOT(T(B)), AND(T(K), T(O))), IFF(EXACTLY(2; T(J); T(O); T(H)), AND(T(P), T(D))))."
- D says: "XOR(EXACTLY(1; T(K); T(I); T(E)), XOR(T(Q), T(N)))."
- E says: "IMPLIES(IFF(XOR(T(P), T(L)), T(H)), XOR(EXACTLY(2; T(Q); T(L); T(M)), AND(T(P), T(N))))."
- F says: "AND(XOR(XOR(T(E), T(J)), XOR(T(N), T(M))), IFF(EXACTLY(1; T(O); T(N); T(A)), NOT(T(G))))."
- G says: "IFF(OR(T(N), EXACTLY(1; T(O); T(E); T(N))), AND(EXACTLY(1; T(Q); T(A); T(B)), EXACTLY(1; T(B); T(H); T(K))))."
- H says: "IMPLIES(OR(T(G), T(O)), EXACTLY(2; T(P); T(A); T(N)))."
- I says: "AND(OR(T(E), T(J)), IMPLIES(XOR(T(G), T(F)), NOT(T(O))))."
- J says: "AND(OR(AND(T(N), T(E)), XOR(T(E), T(D))), IMPLIES(NOT(T(M)), EXACTLY(1; T(O); T(H); T(G))))."
- K says: "IMPLIES(OR(T(B), T(O)), OR(T(Q), T(J)))."
- L says: "XOR(IMPLIES(T(O), XOR(T(K), T(G))), XOR(XOR(T(I), T(B)), T(F)))."
- M says: "AND(T(J), XOR(T(L), T(F)))."
- N says: "IMPLIES(IFF(AND(T(K), T(F)), XOR(T(K), T(E))), OR(EXACTLY(2; T(B); T(K); T(F)), AND(T(M), T(A))))."
- O says: "OR(IMPLIES(OR(T(J), T(P)), EXACTLY(2; T(E); T(Q); T(K))), AND(OR(T(L), T(M)), XOR(T(A), T(B))))."
- P says: "AND(OR(T(C), T(H)), OR(T(C), T(I)))."
- Q says: "AND(IMPLIES(AND(T(O), T(M)), XOR(T(H), T(J))), IFF(T(N), NOT(T(I))))."
